final_prompt.prompt:
Create a brand color palette and typography specification sheet for 染秋 (RAN QIU), an autumn fragrance brand rooted in Eastern philosophy and the dyeing arts, in the warm reverence of 原研哉's restrained editorial identity work.

Style/medium: brand identity specification sheet — clean, editorial, with the matte warmth of dyed silk and handmade paper; structured as a formal brand standards page
Composition: vertical layout divided into three sections — primary color swatches (top), secondary/accent color swatches (middle), typography specimens (bottom); generous breathing room between sections, each swatch labeled with color name and hex/RGB value
Subject: primary palette of deep autumn tones drawn from osmanthus amber, persimmon rust, maple crimson, and pu-erh oolong brown; secondary palette of muted supporting tones — celadon mist, ink graphite, pale rice paper cream; typography section showing brand logotype "染秋 RAN QIU" in a refined serif/brush hybrid, plus body typeface specimens in Chinese and Latin
Scene/backdrop: neutral rice paper cream background, the quiet of an empty studio in late October
Lighting/mood: the diffused warmth of afternoon light through shoji paper — no harsh shadows, only the soft glow of a season turning; mood of quiet reverence and unhurried attention
Color palette: primary — osmanthus amber (#C8813A), persimmon rust (#B85C38), maple crimson (#8B2E16), oolong brown (#5C3D2E); secondary — celadon mist (#A8B5A0), ink graphite (#3A3530), rice paper cream (#F5EFE0); all swatches rendered as rectangular blocks with color name in Chinese and English, hex code below
Materials/textures: matte dyed-silk surface for swatch blocks, subtle paper grain on background, ink-brush stroke as a thin decorative divider between sections
Text (verbatim): 染秋　RAN QIU　桂花乌龙　琥珀枫叶　柿霜暮色　主色　辅色　字体规范
Constraints: all three sections (主色 / 辅色 / 字体规范) must be clearly visible and labeled; color swatches must show both Chinese name and hex value; typography section must include logotype display and body text specimen; layout must read as a formal brand standards document
Avoid: generic Western brand-kit templates, neon or cool-toned colors, overly decorative flourishes that undermine the Eastern restraint, dark background
